honesty: CIM = Alpha 13pts CALIBRATED. SPIKE (2026-06-06) confirms the production Card reproduces the kernel on the same 800MHz AIPU (square M=1,K=2048,N=2048: Card 200.8 vs Alpha 203.7 GOP/s, |diff|=1.4%, no rescale). Full revalidation + prefill GEMM pending (F-gate, user OK).

spike.compiler: v1.6 ships `axcompile` (axelera-devkit 1.6.1, Artifactory, no entitlement block beyond the URL; officially Beta). The old `compile` was renamed, NOT removed (corrects the prior 'no compiler' conclusion).
spike.conv_proxy_compiles: YES via axcompile --input … --input-shape 1,K,1,M --output … --overwrite --dataset-len N (auto-generated INT8 calibration, no imageset).
spike.raw_gemm: raw MatMul/Gemm FAILS to compile (ONNXGraphCleanerError: not topologically sorted) -> the 1x1-conv proxy is required (confirmed).
spike.board: was in a PCIe AER uncorrectable-fault state (kernel auto-recovery failed); recovered via `axdevice --refresh` (pcie-rescan + reload-firmware). Now metis-0:7:0 16GiB clock=800MHz mvm=100%.

next: F-gate (user OK): run run_metis_cim_v16.py full (alpha13 13 pts + prefill M>=128 + matmul_sweep) -> measurements/metis_card/metis_card_matmul.json -> validate_cim_card.py -> CARD_REVALIDATED; then prefill-GEMM fit (the ~80x-mispredict gap) + edge-CIM memory layer.
